name: Ribadesella, Arriondas, Cangas de Onís — circular route
name_original: Ribadesella, Arriondas, Cangas de Onís — ruta circular
region_intro: Cycling routes Ribadesella offer a diverse landscape for touring cyclists, characterized by a rugged coastline, the Sella River estuary, and proximity to the Sierra del Sueve and Picos de Europa mountains. The region features a mix of coastal paths, river valleys, and rolling hills, providing varied terrain. Rural roads with minimal traffic connect charming villages and green meadows, suitable for exploring the Asturian countryside. This combination of coastal and inland features defines the touring cycling experience in Ribadesella. Best touring cycling routes around Ribadesella The most popular touring cycling route is Ribadesella, Arriondas, Cangas de Onís — circular route, a difficult 41.4 miles (66.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 46 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain through inland valleys. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sella Bridge – Ribadesella loop from Ribadesella / Ribeseya, a moderate 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This route follows the Sella River, providing views of the estuary and surrounding areas. Local touring cyclists also love the San Antolín de Bedón Beach – Borizu Beach loop from Ribadesella, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas and beaches, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Touring cycling around Ribadesella is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Ribadesella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ribadesella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ribadesella?
A: Ribadesella offers over 30 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging paths to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ribadesella?
A: The routes around Ribadesella feature a diverse landscape, including rugged coastlines, paths along the Sella River estuary, and rolling hills leading towards the Sierra del Sueve and Picos de Europa mountains. You'll encounter a mix of coastal paths, river valleys, and tranquil rural roads through green meadows and charming villages.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Ribadesella?
A: Yes, Ribadesella offers several routes suitable for families. There are 4 easy routes and 10 moderate routes that provide a less strenuous experience, often following flatter sections along the river or coast. These are great for enjoying the scenery with children.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Cantabrian Sea, dramatic cliffs, and beautiful bays. You can also follow the Sella River, famous for its annual canoe race. For cultural interest, the area is home to prehistoric sites like the Tito Bustillo Cave, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Some routes pass through charming villages with traditional Asturian architecture. You can also find viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the coastline and inland mountains.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Ribadesella area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029987046/ribadesella-arriondas-cangas-de-onis-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella, Arriondas, Cangas de Onís — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 41.4-mile (66.7 km) ride through inland valleys. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sella Bridge – Ribadesella loop from Ribadesella / Ribeseya&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Sella River.

Q: Where can I find parking for cycling routes in Ribadesella?
A: Ribadesella, being a popular outdoor destination, generally offers various parking options, especially near the town center and popular beaches like Santa Marina. For specific route starting points, it's advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps for designated parking areas. Many rural roads also have informal pull-offs.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Ribadesella?
A: The best season for touring cycling in Ribadesella is typically spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with less rainfall and fewer crowds than the peak summer season. Summer can also be enjoyable, but be prepared for warmer temperatures and more tourists, especially along the coast.

Q: Are there bike rental services available in Ribadesella?
A: Yes, there are local businesses in Ribadesella that offer bicycle and electric bicycle rentals. This makes it convenient for visitors who wish to explore the area on two wheels without bringing their own equipment. A quick search for 'bike rental Ribadesella' will provide current options.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Ribadesella?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ribadesella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse scenery, which seamlessly blends stunning coastal views with tranquil river valleys and rolling hills. The variety of routes catering to different fitness levels is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Can I combine cycling with exploring nearby attractions like caves?
A: Absolutely! Ribadesella is famous for its prehistoric caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8017662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to Cueva de Tito Bustillo&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is a significant cultural stop. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cuevona of Cuevas del Agua&lt;/a&gt;, a natural cave that serves as the only entrance to the village of Cuevas del Agua. Several routes pass near these fascinating sites, allowing for a unique blend of cycling and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Picos de Europa mountains?
A: Yes, while Ribadesella is coastal, its proximity to the Picos de Europa means some routes offer glimpses of these imposing mountains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antolín de Bedón Beach – Llanes Marina loop from Ribadesella&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 76.7-mile (123.4 km) route, provides expansive views that can include the Picos de Europa in the distance, especially from higher vantage points.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes for refreshments?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Ribadesella pass through or near charming villages and the main town itself. These locations offer opportunities to stop for refreshments at local cafes, pubs, and restaurants. The rural countryside routes might have fewer options, so it's always a good idea to carry water and snacks, but you'll generally find places to refuel in the more populated areas.
tips: A small town near the Picos de Europa, at the foot of the Sierra del Sueve and a place where the Sella River passes through. There are many companies that organize canoe routes on the river – you can choose between routes of 6, 10 or 14 km or do the complete route of the descent of the Sella.

The Roman bridge of Cangas de Onís, or puentón, is located over the Sella river and is one of the symbols of Asturias. Although it is known as the Roman bridge, it is a medieval construction from the time of the reign of Alfonso XI of Castile with its famous peralted arch and two other minor arches that are unequal. In 1939, a large Victory Cross, symbol of Asturias, was placed in the center of its great central arch.

The church was built in the 13th century and is considered one of the most emblematic monuments of the city. Don Pelayo was an Asturian nobleman who led the resistance against the Muslim invasion of the Iberian Peninsula in the 8th century. He is considered one of Spain's national heroes and is credited with founding the kingdom of Asturias, of which he was its first king. The connection between the Church of the Assumption and Don Pelayo lies in the fact that it is believed that the church was built on the place where the Battle of Covadonga took place, led by Don Pelayo in the year 722, which is considered the first victory of the Christians. on Muslims in the Iberian Peninsula. In this way, the church becomes an important place of pilgrimage for Asturians and for lovers of Spanish history.

The houses of the Indianos have beautiful buildings with a very distinguishable architecture and beautiful colors. The Indians were emigrants who made their fortune in Latin America (Cuba and Mexico, especially). It is also worth seeing the Church of the Holy Cross, the Roman bridge, the Aula del Reino de Asturias, the Parish Church, the Pintu Palace, the Market Square and the Dago House.
Picturesque medieval stone bridge that crosses the Guadamía River.

From this bridge you can see, to the south, the view of the estuary with the mountains in the background and, to the north, the sea and the port. On this bridge the public gathers to cheer the participants of the International Descent of the Sella, also known in Asturias as the Fiesta de Les Piragües, the most popular canoe competition in Spain.

The historic town of Ribadesella was founded by King Alfonso X the Wise in the 13th century. It has an interesting mix of medieval city and modernist architecture as well as an outstanding beach area. The town has two clearly differentiated parts: the east side with the old part of the city, and the west side, home to the Tito Bustillo cave, one of the treasures of European rock art.
